---
title: TPM for personal servers
description: You have encrypted server, its on a battery backup. You're on vacation and power goes out long enough to kill UPS. System boots and hangs at wanting the encrypt
slug: tpm-for-personal-servers
date: 2023-09-21
published: false
tags: []
---

You have encrypted server, its on a battery backup. You're on vacation and power goes out long enough to kill UPS. System boots and hangs at wanting the encryption key.

There are plenty of guides where you can install Dropbear a small SSH server that starts during the boot loader and allows you to put in your ssh key over encryption. I haven't seen many guides where this works with wireguard and even if it did you wireguard private key would still be visible.


---

Verifying TPM

```bash
dmesg | grep -i tpm
```

libtss2-tcti-tabrmd0
tpm2-abrmd

https://paolozaino.wordpress.com/2021/02/21/linux-configure-and-use-your-tpm-2-0-module-on-linux/

https://ericchiang.github.io/post/tpm-keys/

First we create the primary key.

```bash
ncrmro@mox:~$ tpm2 createprimary --key-context test
name-alg:
  value: sha256
  raw: 0xb
attributes:
  value: fixedtpm|fixedparent|sensitivedataorigin|userwithauth|restricted|decrypt
  raw: 0x30072
type:
  value: rsa
  raw: 0x1
exponent: 65537
bits: 2048
scheme:
  value: null
  raw: 0x10
scheme-halg:
  value: (null)
  raw: 0x0
sym-alg:
  value: aes
  raw: 0x6
sym-mode:
  value: cfb
  raw: 0x43
sym-keybits: 128
rsa: 
```

```
tpm2 getrandom 32 --hex
f605f7332a8b2a228f7b47c93695b44206e31960eb66a1a22cb25d6649aa009f
```
